interactions.summary: Jiang answers Amber by saying current society is anti-love: school makes parents deny children's free will and care about grades and elite college admission more than happiness; Amber restates that denial of free will is anti-love and evil.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang in seg-0065 and seg-0066; Amber/student in seg-0067; Jiang confirms in seg-0068.

interactions.summary: Another student asks why power prevents people from approaching the Monad; Jiang answers that power rationalizes itself through rival interpretations, material-world systems, and ideas that preserve control even when they deny truth.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Student in seg-0069 and seg-0071; Jiang in seg-0070 and seg-0072.

speaker_notes.note: Q&A packet; SPEAKER_03 appears to be Amber, SPEAKER_00 another student, and SPEAKER_01 Jiang.

claims.claim: Jiang says contemporary society is anti-love because institutions such as school distort parent-child relationships from happiness and agency toward grades, elite college admission, and future employment anxiety.
Amber accurately restates Jiang's model: denying free will is anti-love, therefore evil, and limits movement toward the Monad.
claims.temporal_scope: Student restatement confirmed in the 2025-08-29 lecture.

claims.claim: Jiang says the powerful maintain power by developing a religion and systems that justify material-world control, even if those systems invert the Monad as evil and deny truth.

signature_moments.moment: School turns parental love into anxiety over grades.
signature_moments.source_phrase: because of school, parents care about your grades
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It grounds the metaphysical model in an ordinary institution students immediately know.

signature_moments.moment: Power does not merely disobey the Monad; it rewrites the map so its own god becomes good.
signature_moments.source_phrase: In their conception, the Monad's the evil one. Their god's the good one.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the strongest explanation of why evil can feel righteous from inside the system.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A society structured so normal human relationships cannot flourish because institutions deny free will and agency.
The concrete form of anti-love in Jiang's answer: replacing a person's agency with institutional demands.

chronology_notes.note: The school/parent claim may connect to earlier schooling and anti-agency materials, but this packet itself only supplies the dated 2025-08-29 formulation.

uncertainty_notes.note: One middle phrase in the school example is ASR-damaged, but the institutional anti-love claim is clear.
